description_long: This is a tall, ornate clock tower reminiscent of the iconic Elizabeth Tower (commonly known as Big Ben) in London. The structure features a rectangular shaft with detailed vertical and horizontal banding, topped by a steep, multi-tiered spire with a blue-gray roof and decorative finials. Four large, circular clock faces are set into the upper section of the tower, each with white backgrounds, black Roman numerals, and black clock hands. The tower is primarily constructed from a reddish-brown stone or brick, with the roof and spire made of a contrasting blue-gray material. The overall design is highly detailed, with architectural embellishments such as cornices, pilasters, and ornamental spires at each corner of the clock section.
description: A tall, reddish-brown clock tower with four ornate clock faces and a blue-gray spire, inspired by the Elizabeth Tower in London. The structure features detailed stonework and decorative architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing two prominent clock faces, detailed stonework, and the full height of the blue-gray spire.
description_view_1: Rotated view revealing the adjacent sides of the tower, with two clock faces visible and more of the spire's structure.
description_view_2: Side view with two clock faces in clear view, highlighting the vertical banding and architectural details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing two clock faces and the ornate spire from a different angle.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock faces are large, circular, and white with black Roman numerals marking the hours. Each face has two black hands (hour and minute), and a decorative border surrounds the face. The design is classic and ornate, matching the historical style of the tower.
attribute_tower_roof_type: steep spire with multiple tiers and finials
